title: silene gallica var. modesta
intro: Silene gallica var. modesta, also known as the modest catchfly, is a perennial plant belonging to the carnation family and native to Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving rheumatic pain, and acting as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Silene gallica var. modesta include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
